payload.title: Spring Production Update

payload.title: Residence Hall Pet Policy
payload.text: Pets are not permitted in Northmoor residence halls, with the exception of registered service animals and approved emotional support animals with documentation.

_debug.note: Off-topic. No conflict with any established fact.

payload.title: Veterans' Services
payload.text: The Veterans' Services Office assists student veterans with GI Bill processing, academic advising, and peer mentoring. The office is located in Hadley Hall, Room 210.

_debug.establishes: Veterans' Services in Hadley Hall Rm 210
_debug.note: Baseline fact. No planned conflict.

payload.title: Tuition Payment Plan
payload.text: Northmoor offers a quarterly tuition payment plan with installments due in August, October, January, and March. A $35 enrollment fee applies.

_debug.note: Baseline fact. No planned conflict.

payload.title: Student Organization Grants
payload.text: Recognized student organizations at Northmoor may apply for annual activity grants of up to two thousand dollars through Student Affairs. Grants support events, supplies, and travel.

_debug.note: Paraphrase of club_funding (seq 53). '$2,000' vs 'two thousand dollars'. Same facts in slightly different wording.

payload.title: Student Mailroom
payload.text: The student mailroom is located in the basement of the Oakwood Student Center and is open Monday through Friday, 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Package pickup requires a valid student ID.

_debug.note: Off-topic. Mentions Oakwood Student Center but no conflict with student_center (seq 35).

payload.title: Homecoming Weekend (Rescheduled)
payload.text: Northmoor's Homecoming weekend has been rescheduled to October 25–27 due to a conflict with the regional athletics conference schedule. The parade and tailgate will proceed as planned on the new dates.

payload.title: Study Data Science at Northmoor

payload.title: University Grading Standards
payload.text: Northmoor employs a standard letter-grade system: A for scores 90 through 100, B for 80 through 89, C for 70 through 79, D for 60 through 69, and F for anything below 60.

_debug.note: Paraphrase of grading_scale (seq 83). Nearly identical content, slightly different phrasing ('scores 90 through 100' vs '90–100').
